NAME

json.h - 📝 JSON structured logging output

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void log_json_write (int fd, log_level_t level, uint64_t time_nanoseconds, const char *file, int line, const char *func, const char *message)
Write a log entry as a JSON object to the json output fd. void log_set_json_output (int fd)
Set the JSON output file descriptor. void log_json_async_safe (int fd, log_level_t level, const char *file, int line, const char *func, const char *message)
Async-safe JSON logging for signal handlers.

Detailed Description

📝 JSON structured logging output

Definition in file include/ascii-chat/log/json.h.

Function Documentation

void log_json_async_safe (int fd, log_level_t level, const char * file, int line, const char * func, const char * message)

Async-safe JSON logging for signal handlers. This function formats and writes JSON logs using ONLY async-safe operations:

  • snprintf for string formatting
  • write() for output
  • No allocations, no locks, no library calls

Suitable for calling from signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGINT, etc.)

Parameters

fd File descriptor to write to (typically STDOUT_FILENO or STDERR_FILENO)
level Log level (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)
file Source file name (can be NULL)
line Source line number (can be 0)
func Function name (can be NULL)
message Log message (must not be NULL)

This function formats and writes JSON logs using ONLY async-safe operations:

  • snprintf for string formatting
  • write() for output
  • No allocations, no locks, no library calls

Suitable for calling from signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGINT, etc.)

Parameters

fd File descriptor to write to (typically STDOUT_FILENO or STDERR_FILENO)
level Log level
file Source file name (may be NULL)
line Source line number
func Function name (may be NULL)
message Log message (must not be NULL)

Definition at line 250 of file json.c.

250                                                                                                                        {
251   if (fd < 0 || !message) {
252     return;
253   }
254 
255   /* Format JSON manually on stack using only async-safe operations */
256   char json_buffer[2048];
257   char escaped_message[512];
258   char escaped_file[256];
259   char escaped_func[128];
260   char timestamp_buf[32];
261 
262   /* Escape the strings (normalize file path to project-relative) */
263   json_escape_async_safe(message, escaped_message, sizeof(escaped_message));
264   if (file) {
265     const char *rel_file = extract_project_relative_path(file);
266     json_escape_async_safe(rel_file, escaped_file, sizeof(escaped_file));
267   } else {
268     escaped_file[0] = '\0';
269   }
270   json_escape_async_safe(func ? func : "", escaped_func, sizeof(escaped_func));
271 
272   /* Format timestamp using only async-safe operations */
273   uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
274   format_timestamp_microseconds(time_ns, timestamp_buf, sizeof(timestamp_buf));
275 
276   /* Get thread ID (note: asciichat_thread_current_id is assumed to be async-safe) */
277   uint64_t tid = (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_current_id();
278 
279   /* Format complete JSON object with timestamp */
280   int written = snprintf(json_buffer, sizeof(json_buffer),
281                          "{\"header\":{\"timestamp\":\"%s\",\"level\":\"%s\",\"tid\":%lu,"
282                          "\"file\":\"%s\",\"line\":%d,\"func\":\"%s\"},"
283                          "\"body\":{\"message\":\"%s\"}}\n",
284                          timestamp_buf, log_level_to_string(level), (unsigned long)tid, file ? escaped_file : "", line,
285                          func ? escaped_func : "", escaped_message);
286 
287   /* Write to fd if successful */
288   if (written > 0 && written < (int)sizeof(json_buffer)) {
289     platform_write_all(fd, (const uint8_t *)json_buffer, (size_t)written);
290   }
291 }




References asciichat_thread_current_id(),
    extract_project_relative_path(), platform_write_all(), and
    time_get_realtime_ns().


Referenced by log_console_impl().






void
  log_json_write (int fd, log_level_t level, uint64_t
  time_nanoseconds, const char * file, int line, const char * func, const char *
  message)


Write a log entry as a JSON object to the json output fd. Outputs
    a newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON) format log entry with the following
    structure:




{


  "header": {


    "timestamp": "14:30:45.123456",


    "level": "INFO",


    "tid": 12345,


    "file": "lib/network/server.c",


    "line": 42,


    "func": "handle_client"


  },


  "body": {


    "message": "Client connected"


  }
}



The file, line, and func fields are omitted from the output when
    they are NULL/0 (which typically occurs in release builds).




Parameters


fd File descriptor to write JSON to (-1 =
  disabled)


level Log level (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)


time_nanoseconds Current time in nanoseconds (used to format timestamp)


file Source file name (can be NULL)


line Line number (can be 0)


func Function name (can be NULL)


message Formatted log message


See also


log_set_json_output() - Set the JSON output file
  descriptor


Definition at line 94 of file json.c.


95                                          {
96   if (fd < 0 || !message) {
97     return;
98   }
99 
100   /* Create root object */
101   yyjson_mut_doc *doc = yyjson_mut_doc_new(NULL);
102   if (!doc) {
103     return;
104   }
105 
106   yyjson_mut_val *root = yyjson_mut_obj(doc);
107   if (!root) {
108     yyjson_mut_doc_free(doc);
109     return;
110   }
111   yyjson_mut_doc_set_root(doc, root);
112 
113   /* Create "header" object */
114   yyjson_mut_val *header = yyjson_mut_obj(doc);
115   if (header) {
116     yyjson_mut_obj_add_val(doc, root, "header", header);
117 
118     /* Add timestamp (HH:MM:SS.microseconds) */
119     char timestamp_buf[32];
120     size_t ts_len = format_timestamp_microseconds(time_nanoseconds, timestamp_buf, sizeof(timestamp_buf));
121     if (ts_len > 0) {
122       yyjson_mut_obj_add_strncpy(doc, header, "timestamp", timestamp_buf, ts_len);
123     }
124 
125     /* Add level */
126     yyjson_mut_obj_add_str(doc, header, "level", log_level_to_string(level));
127 
128     /* Add thread ID */
129     uint64_t tid = (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_current_id();
130     yyjson_mut_obj_add_uint(doc, header, "tid", tid);
131 
132     /* Add file (if not NULL) - normalize to project-relative path */
133     if (file) {
134       const char *rel_file = extract_project_relative_path(file);
135       yyjson_mut_obj_add_str(doc, header, "file", rel_file);
136     }
137 
138     /* Add line (if > 0) */
139     if (line > 0) {
140       yyjson_mut_obj_add_int(doc, header, "line", line);
141     }
142 
143     /* Add func (if not NULL) */
144     if (func) {
145       yyjson_mut_obj_add_str(doc, header, "func", func);
146     }
147   }
148 
149   /* Create "body" object with message */
150   yyjson_mut_val *body = yyjson_mut_obj(doc);
151   if (body) {
152     yyjson_mut_obj_add_val(doc, root, "body", body);
153     yyjson_mut_obj_add_str(doc, body, "message", message);
154   }
155 
156   /* Serialize to compact JSON (single line, no pretty-printing) */
157   size_t json_len = 0;
158   char *json_str = yyjson_mut_write(doc, 0, &json_len);
159 
160   if (json_str && json_len > 0) {
161     /* Write JSON string */
162     platform_write(fd, (const uint8_t *)json_str, json_len);
163 
164     /* Write newline for NDJSON format */
165     platform_write(fd, (const uint8_t *)"\n", 1);
166 
167     /* Free serialized JSON string */
168     free(json_str);
169   }
170 
171   /* Free document */
172   yyjson_mut_doc_free(doc);
173 }






References asciichat_thread_current_id(),
    extract_project_relative_path(), and platform_write().


Referenced by log_msg(), and log_plain_msg().






void
  log_set_json_output (int fd)


Set the JSON output file descriptor. Sets which file descriptor
    should receive JSON-formatted logs. Pass -1 to disable JSON output.




Parameters


fd File descriptor (typically STDERR_FILENO for
  terminal, or an open log file)


Note


This function is thread-safe (uses atomic
  operations)


Definition at line 726 of file log/log.c.


726                                  {
727   atomic_store_int(&g_log.json_file, fd);
728 }






References atomic_store_int().


Referenced by main().
